chapter: Nelson Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom
subject: English

flashcards.back: Apartheid is a political system that separates people according to their race. South Africa had such a system.

flashcards.front: Who fought against Apartheid in South Africa?
flashcards.back: Nelson Mandela and his African National Congress (ANC) spent a lifetime fighting against Apartheid.

flashcards.front: How long did Nelson Mandela spend in prison?
flashcards.back: Nelson Mandela had to spend thirty years in prison for his struggle against Apartheid.

flashcards.front: When were the first democratic elections held in South Africa?
flashcards.back: Democratic elections were held in South Africa in 1994, leading to the end of Apartheid.

flashcards.front: Who became South Africa's first Black President?
flashcards.back: Nelson Mandela became South Africa's first Black President after the 1994 democratic elections.

flashcards.front: What is the title of Nelson Mandela's autobiography from which this extract is taken?
flashcards.back: The extract is from Nelson Mandela's autobiography titled 'Long Walk to Freedom'.

flashcards.front: What historic occasion does Mandela speak about in the extract?
flashcards.back: Mandela speaks about 'the inauguration,' which was his swearing-in ceremony as President.

flashcards.front: On what date did the inauguration ceremony take place?
flashcards.back: The inauguration ceremony took place on 10 May 1994.

flashcards.front: Where did the inauguration ceremony take place?
flashcards.back: The ceremonies took place in the Union Buildings amphitheatre in Pretoria.

flashcards.front: How many seats did Mandela's ANC party win in the 1994 elections?
flashcards.back: Nelson Mandela's African National Congress (ANC) party won 252 of the 400 seats.
Politicians and dignitaries from more than 140 countries around the world attended the ceremony.

flashcards.front: What did Mandela mean by 'political emancipation'?
flashcards.back: Political emancipation refers to freedom from restriction or oppression, specifically from the Apartheid regime.

flashcards.front: What promise did Mandela make regarding future oppression in South Africa?
flashcards.back: He promised that never again would South Africa experience the oppression of one by another.

flashcards.front: What was significant about the location of the inauguration ceremony?
flashcards.back: The Union Buildings had previously been the seat of white supremacy, but now hosted a non-racial government's installation.

flashcards.front: What did 'a rainbow gathering' symbolize at the inauguration?
flashcards.back: It symbolized a beautiful coming together of various peoples and nations, representing unity and diversity in South Africa.
